title: Velvety Ethiopian Shrimp Soup
description: A buttery, lowcalorie shrimp soup infused with traditional Ethiopian berbere spice, silky coconut milk and bright lemon, perfect for a comforting lunch.
instructions: In a bowl, combine shrimp with lemon juice, minced ginger, garlic, berbere, and a pinch of salt; let marinate for 10 minutes.
Heat butter in a large pot over medium heat until melted.
Add diced onion and sauté until translucent, about 3 minutes.
Stir in tomato paste and cook for 1 minute to deepen flavor.
Pour in vegetable broth and bring to a gentle simmer.
Add coconut milk, reduce heat, and let the mixture simmer for 5 minutes to meld the flavors.
Add the marinated shrimp to the pot and cook for 5–7 minutes, until shrimp turn pink and reach 63 °C internal temperature.
Turn off the heat, stir in chopped cilantro, and adjust seasoning if needed.
Serve hot, optionally garnishing with extra cilantro or a squeeze of lemon.
Enjoy your velvety, low‑calorie Ethiopian shrimp soup!
